metric_name: Global 5000 companies with SOA activities underway end of 2006
notes: From Aberdeen's ESB and SOA Middleware study; 90% of Global 5000 will exit 2006 with SOA planning, design, or programming activities underway.

metric_name: Share of survey participants building SOA apps using web services / SOA Lite tools
notes: About half of Aberdeen survey participants building SOA applications using web services and lightweight SOA tools (SOA Lite).
One-third of organizations planning four or more SOA projects in 2006, indicating strong developer enthusiasm.
Aberdeen states IBM WebSphere suite meets definition of Enterprise SOA and is exemplar of what enterprise developers should use.
120-person private company with offices in Austin, TX and Mumbai, India; lacked market footprint to scale independently.

metric_name: IBM revenue at time of Webify acquisition
notes: IBM described as $80 billion technology giant capable of amplifying Webify's technology globally.

metric_value: Insurance, healthcare, banking, telecom, public sector (in development)
notes: Initial release covers insurance and healthcare; banking, telecom, public sector under development.
Aberdeen states IBM with Webify is in a class of its own for vertical-market SOA application components.

metric_name: HP competitive gap in vertical SOA
metric_value: 3 years of unutilized vertical process models
notes: HP has had outstanding vertical process models on shelf for 3 years but missing from competitive playing field; Mercury Interactive acquisition provides new SOA infrastructure.
Oracle's process-model-driven Fusion applications expected next year (2007); represents future competition to IBM-Webify.

metric_value: May gain consideration following IBM blessing of vertical SOA customization concept
notes: Aberdeen suggests Vitria's vertical applications may benefit from IBM legitimizing the vertical SOA approach.

metric_name: Aberdeen recommendation for enterprise application developers
metric_value: Evaluate IBM-Webify vs. Eclipse and Visual Studio on lifecycle lines of code metric
notes: Aberdeen recommends healthcare and insurance enterprise programmers evaluate IBM-Webify vs. general-purpose IDEs; metric is lifecycle lines of code written.

metric_name: SOA architecture impact on lifecycle costs
metric_value: Proving to lower lifecycle costs vs. prior approaches
notes: Aberdeen Research Brief (June 2006) conclusion: SOA decomposition of business processes proving to lower lifecycle costs.

metric_name: IBM services organization benefit from Webify
metric_value: Enables boardroom-to-CIO conversations across business transformation, time-to-market, and software lifecycle costs
notes: Webify gives IBM's vertically-oriented global services organization a powerful product set for multi-level sales discussions.

metric_value: Composite of packaged and homegrown applications for foreseeable future; not total rewrite
notes: Aberdeen Research Brief conclusion: for vast majority of organizations SOA services will span composite of packaged and homegrown applications.
